question.text: A person found guilty of fraudulent trade practices under Section 15HA earns a profit of Rs. 1 crore. What is the applicable penalty range?

answer.text: Minimum Rs. 1 lakh, maximum Rs. 1 crore
Minimum Rs. 10 lakh, maximum Rs. 25 crore or 3x profits
Minimum Rs. 5 lakh, maximum Rs. 25 crore or 3x profits (i.e., Rs. 3 crore), whichever is higher
Minimum Rs. 5 lakh, maximum Rs. 10 crore

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15HA prescribes a minimum penalty of Rs. 5 lakh and maximum of Rs. 25 crore or 3 times the profits, whichever is higher. With a Rs. 1 crore profit, 3x = Rs. 3 crore. Since Rs. 25 crore > Rs. 3 crore, the maximum penalty is Rs. 25 crore. The range is Rs. 5 lakh to Rs. 25 crore. **The Trap:** The minimum of Rs. 10 lakh applies to insider trading (Section 15G), not fraudulent trade practices. Rs. 1 lakh minimum is for general contraventions (Section 15HB). **Cross-Exam Context:** Distinguishing between penalty sections (15G, 15HA, 15HB) is critical for NISM Series IX and Series XII exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For 15HA, the 3x profits clause only matters when profits exceed Rs. 8.33 crore (since 3 × 8.33 = Rs. 25 crore). Below that threshold, the Rs. 25 crore cap is the binding maximum.
